Introduction.Background: This Detailed Diagnostic Report is prepared based on the inspection of a residential flat.
Introduction.Objective: To assess the structural and thermal conditions of the property, identify sources of leakage, dampness, and other defects, and provide recommendations for remediation.
Introduction.Scope_of_Work: Hall, Bedroom, Kitchen, Master Bedroom, Parking Area, Common bathroom, WC, External Wall.
Introduction.Tools_Used: GTC 400 C Professional Thermal Camera, Visual Inspection

Impacted_Areas.Area: Master bedroom wall, External wall and Duct
Impacted_Areas.Positive_Side_Inputs: External wall crack and Duct Issue
Common Bathroom tile hollowness and plumbing issue
Flat no 203 tile joint open and outlet Leakage

Items.Question: Condition of leakage at adjacent walls
Condition of leakage below WC
Leakage during:
Leakage due to concealed plumbing
Leakage due to damage in Nahani trap/Brickbat coba under tile flooring
Gaps/Blackish dirt Observed in tile joints
Gaps around Nahani Trap Joints
Tiles Broken/Loosed anywhere
Loose Plumbing joints/rust around joints and edges (Flush Tank/shower/angle cock/bibcock, washbasin, etc)
Type of tile
Condition of leakage at interior side
Leakage during:
Leakage due to concealed plumbing
Internal WC/Bath/Balcony leakage observed
Existing type of paint and manufacturer
Condition of cracks observed on RCC Column and Beam
Condition of rust marks observed in RCC Beam and Column
Condition of corrosion/spalling of concrete/exposed reinforcement observed in column/beams/roof slab ceiling

Are there any major or minor cracks observed over external surface?
Are the sealants applied on the window frame joints intact?

Are the openings around the pipes in external wall properly grouted? If not its condition.
Condition of any vegetation growth, dish antennas fixed on parapet wall observed?
Chalking and flaking in paint film.
Algae fungus and Moss observed on external wall?

Condition of corrosion on metal rods and MS window grills
Patchwork plaster required? if yes its condition?
Entire replaster required? if yes its condition?
Condition of separation cracks observed at beam-column junction
Condition of leakage observed from overhead water tank
Loose plaster/hollow sond on external surfaces?if observed,

Final_Summary_Table.Observed_Issue: Observed dampness at the skirting level of Hall of Flat No. 103
Final_Summary_Table.Probable_Cause: Observed gaps between the tile joints of Common Bathroom of Flat No. 103
Final_Summary_Table.Suggested_Remedy: Epoxy injection grouting of tile joints in Common Bathroom of Flat No. 103.

Final_Summary_Table.Observed_Issue: Observed dampness at the skirting level of the Common Bedroom of Flat No. 103
Final_Summary_Table.Probable_Cause: Observed gaps between the tile joints of Common Bathroom of Flat No. 103
Final_Summary_Table.Suggested_Remedy: Epoxy injection grouting of tile joints in Common Bathroom of Flat No. 103.

Final_Summary_Table.Observed_Issue: Observed dampness at the skirting level of Master Bedroom of Flat No. 103
Final_Summary_Table.Probable_Cause: Observed gaps between the tile joints of Master Bedroom Bathroom of Flat No. 103
Final_Summary_Table.Suggested_Remedy: Epoxy injection grouting of tile joints in Master Bedroom Bathroom of Flat No. 103.

Final_Summary_Table.Observed_Issue: Observed dampness at the skirting level of Kitchen of Flat No. 103
Final_Summary_Table.Probable_Cause: Observed gaps between the tile joints of Master Bedroom Bathroom of Flat No. 103
Final_Summary_Table.Suggested_Remedy: Epoxy injection grouting of tile joints in Master Bedroom Bathroom of Flat No. 103.

Final_Summary_Table.Observed_Issue: Observed dampness & efflorescence on the wall surface of Master Bedroom of Flat No. 103
Final_Summary_Table.Probable_Cause: Observed cracks on the External wall of building near Master Bedroom of Flat No. 103
Final_Summary_Table.Suggested_Remedy: Polyurethane sealant application for external wall crack repair near Master Bedroom of Flat No. 103.

Final_Summary_Table.Observed_Issue: Observed leakage at the Parking ceiling below Flat No. 103
Final_Summary_Table.Probable_Cause: Observed plumbing issue & gaps between the tile joints of Common Bathroom of Flat No. 103
Final_Summary_Table.Suggested_Remedy: Concealed plumbing leak detection and repair in Common Bathroom of Flat No. 103, followed by epoxy injection grouting of tile joints.

Final_Summary_Table.Impacted_Area: Common Bathroom of Flat No. 103, Flat No. 203
Final_Summary_Table.Observed_Issue: Observed mild dampness at the ceiling of Common Bathroom of Flat No. 103
Final_Summary_Table.Probable_Cause: Observed gap between tile joints of Common & Master Bedroom Bathrooms of Flat No. 203.
Final_Summary_Table.Suggested_Remedy: Epoxy injection grouting of tile joints in Common & Master Bedroom Bathrooms of Flat No. 203 to resolve ceiling dampness.

Overall_Recommendations: Address all identified tile joint gaps in Common and Master Bedroom bathrooms of Flat No. 103 and Flat No. 203 with epoxy injection grouting to prevent water ingress.
Investigate and repair the concealed plumbing issue in the Common Bathroom of Flat No. 103 to resolve active leakage in the Parking Area.
Repair external wall cracks near the Master Bedroom of Flat No. 103 using appropriate polyurethane sealants to prevent moisture penetration.
Monitor and address any future signs of dampness or efflorescence promptly to maintain structural integrity and indoor air quality.
Consider re-evaluating the external wall for additional waterproofing measures if cracks and algae growth persist.
Conduct regular inspections of plumbing systems and tile joints to prevent recurrence of leakage issues.
